name: QAD Manufacturing ERP REST API
what_it_does: QAD Manufacturing ERP REST API for automotive, life sciences, food and beverage, high-tech, and industrial manufacturers to manage production operations, supply chain, financials, customer management, and compliance through QAD's Adaptive ERP cloud platform — enabling automated manufacturing execution, demand planning, supplier collaboration, quality management, and global compliance through QAD's industry-specific ERP built for manufacturers. Enables AI agents to manage production order for manufacturing execution automation, handle materials requirement planning for supply chain demand automation, access supplier portal for vendor collaboration automation, retrieve quality management for manufacturing compliance automation, manage financials for multi-currency manufacturing accounting automation, handle demand planning for inventory optimization automation, access customer management for order-to-cash manufacturing automation, retrieve global trade for import/export compliance automation, manage warehouse for manufacturing inventory management automation, and integrate QAD with MES, WMS, and PLM systems for end-to-end manufacturing operations automation.
use_cases: Automating production order management and scheduling for automotive tier-1 supplier operations automation agents
Managing demand planning and materials requirements planning for food and beverage manufacturer automation agents
Integrating QAD with supplier portal for automotive supply chain collaboration automation agents
Accessing quality and compliance data for life sciences manufacturer GMP automation agents
not_for: Non-manufacturing businesses (QAD is purpose-built for discrete and process manufacturing)
Small businesses (QAD targets mid-market to enterprise manufacturers)
Retail or service industries (QAD is manufacturing-sector-specific ERP)
best_when: A mid-market or enterprise manufacturer in automotive, life sciences, food and beverage, or high-tech wanting AI agents to automate production operations, supply chain, and compliance through QAD's Adaptive ERP cloud platform.
avoid_when: IMPLEMENTATION PROJECT REQUIRED: QAD is an enterprise ERP requiring implementation partner; automated instant ERP API access assumption creates implementation_required; automated must complete QAD implementation project before production API use. MANUFACTURING-SECTOR SPECIFIC: QAD is built for discrete and process manufacturing; automated general-purpose ERP assumption misses manufacturing-specific data models; automated must understand QAD's manufacturing-centric configuration. TENANT-SPECIFIC CONFIGURATION: Each QAD customer has unique ERP configuration; automated generic API assumption creates tenant-specific field differences; automated must understand specific manufacturer's QAD configuration. API ACCESS THROUGH PARTNER CHANNEL: QAD REST API access is managed through QAD partner/customer program; automated open developer API assumption creates no endpoint access; automated must establish QAD customer or partner relationship.

auth.notes: QAD uses OAuth2 for Adaptive ERP REST API authentication. REST API with JSON. Santa Barbara, CA HQ. Founded 1979 by Pamela Lopker. Products: QAD Adaptive ERP, QAD DynaSys (demand planning), QAD EQMS (quality), QAD Supplier Portal, QAD Adaptive Applications. SDKs: None public. Industry focus: automotive, life sciences, food/beverage, high-tech, industrial. Cloud-native (Google Cloud). Acquired by Thoma Bravo (PE). Competes with Infor CloudSuite and Epicor for manufacturing-sector ERP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox for implementation partners; production requires QAD customer contract
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual SaaS subscription per user; module-based licensing; implementation services; support tiers
pricing.notes: Santa Barbara CA. Thoma Bravo PE. Per-user SaaS. Manufacturing ERP. Automotive/life sciences focus.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; transaction and planning APIs throttled per tenant tier

known_agent_gotchas: MANUFACTURING DATA MODEL IS COMPLEX: QAD has a deep manufacturing-specific data model (BOMs, routings, work orders, MRP); automated generic ERP assumption creates missing_field for manufacturing-specific entities; automated must understand QAD's manufacturing data structures

TENANT CONFIGURATION VARIES SIGNIFICANTLY: Each QAD customer configures ERP for their specific industry/processes; automated uniform API assumption creates field_not_found for customer-specific configurations; automated must map each customer's QAD configuration

MRP RUNS ARE BATCH PROCESSES: QAD materials requirements planning runs as batch process; automated real-time MRP assumption creates no immediate planning results; automated must trigger MRP run and poll for completion

AUTOMOTIVE EDI INTEGRATION: QAD automotive customers often require EDI (830, 862 schedules) alongside REST API; automated REST-only automotive assumption misses EDI requirements for automotive customers; automated must implement EDI alongside REST API

MULTI-SITE MANUFACTURING COMPLEXITY: QAD supports multi-site, multi-entity, multi-currency manufacturing; automated single-site assumption creates site_context_required for multi-site manufacturers; automated must specify manufacturing site context for each API call
agent_readiness.security_notes: Manufacturing ERP. ISO-13485, IATF-16949, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU/APAC. Production data, BOMs, and supply chain data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Manufacturing ERP. ISO-13485, FDA 21 CFR. OAuth2. US/EU/APAC. Production and supply chain data.
